2026-04-18T09:23:15.1003991Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-18T09:23:15.1004556Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-18T09:23:15.1004984Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-18T09:23:15.1005356Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-18T09:23:15.1005667Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-18T09:23:15.1005907Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-18T09:23:15.1006073Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-18T09:23:15.1006259Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-18T09:23:15.1007438Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

2026-04-18T09:26:13.7781263Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-18T09:23:18.157398146Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"tools":[{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get a specific label from a repository."},"description":"Get a specific label from a repository.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"name":{"description":"Label name.","type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner (username or organization name)","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo","name"],"type":"object"},"name":"get_label"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Get issue details"},"description":"Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"issue_number":{"description":"The number of the issue","type":"number"},"method":{"description":"The read operation to perform on a single issue.\nOptions are:\n1. get - Get details of a specific issue.\n2. get_comments - Get issue comments.\n3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.\n4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.\n","enum":["get","get_comments","get_sub_issues","get_labels"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"The owner of the repository","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"The name of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["method","owner","repo","issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"issue_read"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List available issue types"},"description":"List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"owner":{"description":"The organization owner of the repository","type":"string"}},"required":["owner"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issue_types"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"List issues"},"description":"List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"after":{"description":"Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.","type":"string"},"direction":{"description":"Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}

2026-04-18T09:26:13.7878978Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-18T09:24:20.081843828Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58327,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Responses README missing x-agent-session-id header, internal activity source, and new observability features\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58314 (commit 54ef6f748) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58314 added several significant features and breaking changes to the AgentServer Responses library:\\n- `x-agent-session-id` response header is now returned on **all** Responses protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) per spec §8\\n- Deterministic SHA-256-based session ID derivation (matching Python SDK: `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` → 63-char hex)\\n- `ResponsesActivitySource` and `ResponsesTracingConstants` were made **internal** (breaking change for users who referenced them for custom spans)\\n- Startup configuration logging via `ResponsesStartupLogger` (storage provider type, default model, fetch history count, event stream TTL)\\n- Chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints\\n- Isolation key presence logging (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey`) on all endpoint logs (values are never logged)\\n- Foundry storage URL masking in diagnostic logs\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README does not document the `x-agent-session-id` response header that clients will now receive on every protocol endpoint — users building client code against this server need to know to expect and optionally consume this header\\n- The README troubleshooting/logging section still implies `ResponsesActivitySource` is publicly usable (\\u0026#34;emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source\\u0026#34;) but does not note it is now internal — any handler author who was referencing this type directly will hit a compile error with no guidance\\n- The deterministic session ID derivation (SHA-256 of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;`) is not documented in the README; this is important for operators who need to understand how session affinity works across requests\\n- The startup configuration logging (what the server logs at startup, and which fields are masked for security) is absent from the README troubleshooting section\\n- Chat isolation key enforcement behavior (mismatched key returns 404 indistinguishable from not-found) is not documented in the README Key Concepts or Troubleshooting sections\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit\\n\\n**1a. Add `x-agent-session-id` response header to Key Concepts**\\n\\nAfter the \\u0026#34;### Thread safety\\u0026#34; subsection, add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Session ID Response Header\\n\\nEvery Responses protocol endpoint — POST, GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems — includes an\\n`x-agent-session-id` response header per the agent server specification §8. The value is the\\nresolved session ID for the request, derived in the following priority order:\\n\\n1. `agent_session_id` from the POST request body\\n2. `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable\\n3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` (63 lowercase hex chars), where the partition hint is extracted from `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id`\\n4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available\\n\\nClients that need to maintain session affinity across a multi-turn conversation can read this header from the POST response and supply it as `agent_session_id` in subsequent requests.\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section**\\n\\nReplace:\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues.\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry distributed traces using the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source name. The `ResponsesActivitySource` class is internal to the framework; do not reference it directly in handler code.\\n\\n**Startup logging:** When the host starts, `ResponsesStartupLogger` logs the active configuration at `Information` level:\\n- Storage provider type (e.g., `FoundryStorageProvider` or `InMemoryResponsesProvider`)\\n- Default model\\n- Default fetch history count\\n- Event stream TTL\\n\\n**Endpoint diagnostic logging:** All Responses endpoints log at `Information` level with response ID, conversation ID, isolation key presence (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey` — key values are never logged), and final status. 4xx/5xx responses from inbound request logging are at `Warning` level.\\n\\n**Foundry storage logs:** Outbound Foundry storage API calls are logged with method, masked URI (everything before `/storage` is redacted; query parameters are stripped except `api-version`), status code, and correlation headers.\\n\\nEnable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to surface these messages.\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Add isolation key enforcement to Troubleshooting / Common errors**\\n\\nAfter the existing 404 Not Found bullet, add:\\n```markdown\\n- **404 Not Found** (isolation mismatch): If a response was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls for that response must include the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns 404 intentionally — this is indistinguishable from not-found to protect tenant isolation.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are needed for this documentation update — the changes are prose-only additions to existing sections.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The additions above slot into existing subsections. No structural changes are needed.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — verify the README diff shows only the prose additions above (no snippet regions should have changed)\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24601560857/agentic_workflow) · ● 343.5K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58327\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-18T09:21:45Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-18T09:21:45Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

2026-04-18T09:26:13.8033047Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-18T09:24:31.371141702Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58327,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Responses README missing x-agent-session-id header, internal activity source, and new observability features\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58314 (commit 54ef6f748) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58314 added several significant features and breaking changes to the AgentServer Responses library:\\n- `x-agent-session-id` response header is now returned on **all** Responses protocol endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) per spec §8\\n- Deterministic SHA-256-based session ID derivation (matching Python SDK: `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` → 63-char hex)\\n- `ResponsesActivitySource` and `ResponsesTracingConstants` were made **internal** (breaking change for users who referenced them for custom spans)\\n- Startup configuration logging via `ResponsesStartupLogger` (storage provider type, default model, fetch history count, event stream TTL)\\n- Chat isolation key enforcement across all endpoints\\n- Isolation key presence logging (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey`) on all endpoint logs (values are never logged)\\n- Foundry storage URL masking in diagnostic logs\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README does not document the `x-agent-session-id` response header that clients will now receive on every protocol endpoint — users building client code against this server need to know to expect and optionally consume this header\\n- The README troubleshooting/logging section still implies `ResponsesActivitySource` is publicly usable (\\u0026#34;emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source\\u0026#34;) but does not note it is now internal — any handler author who was referencing this type directly will hit a compile error with no guidance\\n- The deterministic session ID derivation (SHA-256 of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;`) is not documented in the README; this is important for operators who need to understand how session affinity works across requests\\n- The startup configuration logging (what the server logs at startup, and which fields are masked for security) is absent from the README troubleshooting section\\n- Chat isolation key enforcement behavior (mismatched key returns 404 indistinguishable from not-found) is not documented in the README Key Concepts or Troubleshooting sections\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit\\n\\n**1a. Add `x-agent-session-id` response header to Key Concepts**\\n\\nAfter the \\u0026#34;### Thread safety\\u0026#34; subsection, add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Session ID Response Header\\n\\nEvery Responses protocol endpoint — POST, GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems — includes an\\n`x-agent-session-id` response header per the agent server specification §8. The value is the\\nresolved session ID for the request, derived in the following priority order:\\n\\n1. `agent_session_id` from the POST request body\\n2. `FOUNDRY_AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable\\n3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash of `\\u0026#34;{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}\\u0026#34;` (63 lowercase hex chars), where the partition hint is extracted from `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id`\\n4. Random 63-character hex string when no conversational context is available\\n\\nClients that need to maintain session affinity across a multi-turn conversation can read this header from the POST response and supply it as `agent_session_id` in subsequent requests.\\n```\\n\\n**1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section**\\n\\nReplace:\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues.\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry distributed traces using the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source name. The `ResponsesActivitySource` class is internal to the framework; do not reference it directly in handler code.\\n\\n**Startup logging:** When the host starts, `ResponsesStartupLogger` logs the active configuration at `Information` level:\\n- Storage provider type (e.g., `FoundryStorageProvider` or `InMemoryResponsesProvider`)\\n- Default model\\n- Default fetch history count\\n- Event stream TTL\\n\\n**Endpoint diagnostic logging:** All Responses endpoints log at `Information` level with response ID, conversation ID, isolation key presence (`HasUserIsolationKey`, `HasChatIsolationKey` — key values are never logged), and final status. 4xx/5xx responses from inbound request logging are at `Warning` level.\\n\\n**Foundry storage logs:** Outbound Foundry storage API calls are logged with method, masked URI (everything before `/storage` is redacted; query parameters are stripped except `api-version`), status code, and correlation headers.\\n\\nEnable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to surface these messages.\\n```\\n\\n**1c. Add isolation key enforcement to Troubleshooting / Common errors**\\n\\nAfter the existing 404 Not Found bullet, add:\\n```markdown\\n- **404 Not Found** (isolation mismatch): If a response was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header, all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls for that response must include the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns 404 intentionally — this is indistinguishable from not-found to protect tenant isolation.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are needed for this documentation update — the changes are prose-only additions to existing sections.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). The additions above slot into existing subsections. No structural changes are needed.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — verify the README diff shows only the prose additions above (no snippet regions should have changed)\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt;
